label: Probabilistic & differentiable programming

node.label: Probabilistic & differentiable programming
node.definition: Probabilistic programming expresses generative assumptions as executable programs and delegates posterior inference to sampling, variational, or differentiable machinery.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Probabilistic & differentiable programming is modeled as depending on Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Bayesian & approximate inference and feeding downstream nodes such as Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins, Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins
atlas downstream link to Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Bayesian & approximate inference.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Probabilistic & differentiable programming has a `represents_with` dependency on Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations with `core` strength.

In this atlas graph, Probabilistic & differentiable programming has an `uses` dependency on Bayesian & approximate inference because Bayesian & approximate inference is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
Probabilistic & differentiable programming is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Reparameterization & differentiable sampling.
In this atlas graph, Probabilistic & differentiable programming has a `composes` dependency on Constraint propagation, logic & program execution with `common` strength.
